name: Thurston Law Firm
description_short: Chicago-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings. Christine Thurston personally handles cases across eight Illinois counties with a focus on personalized representation.
description_long:
Thurston Law Firm is a Chicago-based bankruptcy law practice founded by Christine Thurston, a bankruptcy attorney with over a decade of experience in consumer finance law. Thurston began her career in New Jersey handling general practice matters including personal injury, real estate, and family law, before specializing in bankruptcy. She worked for one of Illinois's largest consumer bankruptcy firms handling thousands of cases, then served as in-house counsel for a company litigating bankruptcy, foreclosure, and eviction matters. This dual perspective—having practiced from both debtor and creditor sides—informs her current approach.

The firm offers Chapter 7 bankruptcy (complete debt elimination and fresh start) and Chapter 13 bankruptcy (debt restructuring and repayment plans over time). They assist clients in eliminating credit card debt, medical bills, foreclosure and repossession deficiencies, payday loans, personal loans, and older tax debt. Services include preventing home foreclosure and vehicle repossession, stopping wage garnishments, halting credit harassment and collections, addressing suspended licenses and parking boot issues, preventing student loan garnishments and tax refund interception, and credit score recovery through debt erasure. The firm serves Cook, DuPage, Lake, Kendall, Kane, LaSalle, Grundy, and Will counties, with physical locations in downtown Chicago, Schaumburg, Burr Ridge, Countryside, Naperville, Downers Grove, and St. Charles.

Thurston emphasizes personalized service as their primary differentiator, contrasting themselves explicitly against "bankruptcy mills" where clients interact with legal assistants rather than attorneys. Christine personally handles cases from start to finish and is directly accessible via phone, text, or email. Her philosophy centers on explaining the bankruptcy process thoroughly so clients understand each step with no surprises, maintaining a non-judgmental approach during what she acknowledges is an inherently stressful period. The firm highlights client testimonials and positions itself as accessible and compassionate.

Limitations based on available information: no pricing is disclosed on the website, no information about acceptance rates or specific financial thresholds is provided, and the firm's track record with Chapter 13 vs. Chapter 7 outcomes is not detailed. While the website emphasizes Thurston's trial experience in bankruptcy litigation, specific case results or settlement amounts are not disclosed. The firm does not appear to offer debt counseling, credit repair, or credit monitoring services—they are exclusively a legal filing firm.
address: 141 W Jackson Blvd UNIT 2800, Chicago, IL 60604

company_info.headquarters: 141 W Jackson Blvd UNIT 2800, Chicago, IL 60604

pros: Attorney personally handles all cases from initial consultation through completion, not delegated to paralegals or other attorneys
Dual expertise practicing bankruptcy from both debtor and creditor perspectives, creating more strategic understanding of opposing positions
Lead trial attorney with documented experience winning verdicts and settlements in bankruptcy litigation matters
Multiple office locations across eight Illinois counties (Cook, DuPage, Lake, Kendall, Kane, LaSalle, Grundy, Will) plus remote service capability
Accessible via multiple channels: phone calls, text messages, and email for ongoing client communication
Transparent comparison against 'bankruptcy mills,' explicitly positioning for personalized service versus assembly-line processing
Free bankruptcy consultation with no apparent obligation, allowing clients to understand options before committing
cons: No pricing information disclosed on website—clients must contact firm to learn fees, which may vary by case complexity
No clear information about acceptance criteria or which cases the firm declines, creating uncertainty for prospective clients
Limited detail on Chapter 13 vs. Chapter 7 success rates, discharge timelines, or typical outcomes for different debt profiles
Website design appears outdated with formatting issues ('WHAT OUR CLIENTS SA Y' appears truncated) that may signal less rigorous quality control
No mention of payment plan options, financing arrangements, or accessibility for clients unable to pay attorney fees upfront
best_for: Illinois residents in Cook, DuPage, Lake, Kendall, Kane, LaSalle, Grundy, or Will counties facing foreclosure, repossession, or wage garnishment
Consumers with significant unsecured debt (credit cards, medical bills, payday loans) seeking legal discharge rather than settlement
Debtors preferring direct attorney communication and personalized case handling over standardized firm processing
Small business owners or individuals with complex debt portfolios requiring bankruptcy litigation experience
diagnosis: Thurston Law Firm is best for Illinois residents in specified counties who need professional legal representation for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and value direct attorney communication over assembly-line processing. The primary caveat is that all pricing, acceptance criteria, and specific case outcomes are not publicly disclosed, requiring direct consultation to assess fit and affordability—clients should verify they can afford attorney fees before committing to representation.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing (complete debt elimination and fresh start)
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing (restructured repayment plans over 3-5 years)
Payday loan and personal loan elimination
Suspended driver's license reinstatement assistance via parking ticket bankruptcy payment plans
